spots.title: Sensō-ji Temple
spots.description: Tokyo's oldest temple, framed by the giant Kaminarimon lantern and a lane of century-old snack stalls.

spots.title: Meiji Jingū
spots.description: A vast evergreen forest in the heart of the city, leading to one of Japan's grandest Shinto shrines.

spots.title: teamLab Planets
spots.description: Wade barefoot through water and infinite mirrored rooms in this walk-through digital art museum.

spots.title: Shibuya Sky
spots.description: An open-air rooftop 230m up — the whole city, and on clear evenings Mt. Fuji, glowing at golden hour.

spots.title: Tokyo Tower
spots.description: The orange-and-white lattice icon of the skyline — corny, beloved, and genuinely magical when it lights up.

spots.title: Tokyo Skytree
spots.description: Japan's tallest structure — a 634m broadcast tower with decks that make even Shibuya look tiny.

spots.title: Imperial Palace East Gardens
spots.description: The old Edo Castle grounds — moats, stone walls and manicured gardens in the exact centre of the city.

spots.title: Tsukiji Outer Market
spots.description: The old fish market's outer lanes still run hot — tamagoyaki stands, knife shops and the city's best sushi breakfast.

spots.title: Ueno Park
spots.description: Tokyo's grand old park — a museum quarter, a lotus pond and pandas, all on one hill.

spots.title: Akihabara Electric Town
spots.description: Floor after floor of anime, retro games and electronics under a canopy of neon — otaku heaven, unfiltered.

spots.title: Ginza
spots.description: Tokyo's most polished mile — flagship stores, backstreet kissaten and window displays treated like art.

spots.title: Shinjuku Gyoen
spots.description: Three gardens in one — Japanese, French and English lawns — a genuinely quiet green world off Shinjuku's chaos.

spots.title: Takeshita Street
spots.description: Harajuku's teen-culture artery — crepe stands, purikura booths and fashion at maximum volume.

spots.title: Odaiba Seaside Park
spots.description: A man-made beach with the Rainbow Bridge and city skyline across the bay — Tokyo doing its best seaside impression.

spots.title: Yanaka Ginza
spots.description: The Tokyo that survived — a low-rise shopping street of croquette stands, cats and craftsman shops.

spots.title: Omoide Yokochō
spots.description: A smoky lantern-lit alley of ten-seat yakitori counters pressed against the train tracks — postwar Tokyo, still serving.

spots.title: Nakameguro Canal
spots.description: A tree-lined canal strolled slowly — indie boutiques, canalside coffee and Tokyo's prettiest cherry tunnel in spring.

spots.title: Shinjuku Golden Gai
spots.description: Six alleys, two hundred bars, five seats each — pick a door, squeeze in, and talk to strangers.

spots.title: Daikanyama T-Site
spots.description: A bookstore built like a village — magazines, vinyl and a lounge you'll lose an afternoon to, in Tokyo's calmest fashionable quarter.

spots.title: Blue Bottle Coffee Kiyosumi-Shirakawa
spots.description: The roastery that kicked off Tokyo's third-wave boom, in a warehouse quarter that's become the city's coffee district.

spots.title: Shimokitazawa
spots.description: A tangle of lanes stuffed with vintage shops, record stores and tiny cafés — Tokyo's thrift-and-theatre town.

spots.title: Omotesandō
spots.description: A zelkova-lined runway of flagship architecture — every fashion house built a landmark here, and the backstreets hide the good coffee.

spots.title: Kuramae
spots.description: Old toy-wholesale blocks turned maker district — bean-to-bar chocolate, custom notebooks and riverside coffee.

spots.title: Jiyūgaoka
spots.description: Tokyo's sweets town — patisseries and lifestyle shops on quiet lanes, with a hidden thatched tea house.

spots.title: Kagurazaka
spots.description: Cobbled geisha lanes turned Franco-Japanese quarter — stone alleys, standing sake bars and serious bakeries.

spots.title: Kōenji
spots.description: Shimokita's scrappier sibling — arcades of vintage, record bars and zine culture under the train tracks.

spots.title: Tomigaya
spots.description: The neighborhood behind Yoyogi Park where Tokyo's coffee scene actually lives — Nordic roasters, brunch counters and small bookshops.

spots.title: Sangenjaya
spots.description: Retro alleys and neighborhood coffee a short tram-and-train hop from Shibuya — where locals actually hang out.

spots.title: 21_21 Design Sight
spots.description: Issey Miyake and Tadao Andō's half-buried concrete gallery — one perfectly curated design exhibition at a time.

spots.title: Ebisu
spots.description: Grown-up Shibuya — specialty coffee counters, a photography museum and one of the city's best after-work yokochō.

spots.title: Todoroki Valley
spots.description: Tokyo's only ravine — a shaded stream walk under the city that ends at a temple and a waterfall.

spots.title: Inokashira Park
spots.description: A lake park with swan boats and buskers, attached to Tokyo's most livable neighborhood — and Ghibli's museum.

spots.title: Rikugien Gardens
spots.description: An Edo-period strolling garden built around a poem — Tokyo's most beautiful autumn light-up.

spots.title: Hamarikyu Gardens
spots.description: A shogun's tidal garden where skyscrapers rise straight out of the pines — tea on an island in the pond.

spots.title: Showa Memorial Park
spots.description: A vast national park made for renting bikes — ginkgo tunnels, flower fields and actual open sky.

spots.title: Mount Takao
spots.description: The city's mountain — an hour from Shinjuku, a cable car halfway, and Fuji on the horizon from the top.

spots.title: Koishikawa Kōrakuen
spots.description: One of Tokyo's two oldest gardens — bridges, plum groves and borrowed scenery beside the Dome.

spots.title: Institute for Nature Study
spots.description: A slice of untouched Musashino forest left to grow wild inside the city — capped visitors, real silence.

spots.title: Kasai Rinkai Park
spots.description: Tokyo Bay's big seaside park — a giant Ferris wheel, a bird sanctuary and the city's most underrated sunset.

spots.title: Jindai Botanical Gardens
spots.description: Rose terraces and greenhouse jungles next to Tokyo's second-oldest temple — finish with soba at the gate.

spots.title: Yoyogi Park
spots.description: Tokyo's social lawn — rockabilly dancers, weekend festivals and picnic blankets from edge to edge.

spots.title: Kabukichō
spots.description: Japan's loudest square kilometre — neon canyons, giant Godzilla, and every kind of night you can imagine.

spots.title: Ebisu Yokochō
spots.description: A covered alley of twenty counters under one roof — order from three at once and make friends doing it.

spots.title: Shinjuku Ni-chōme
spots.description: The beating heart of queer Tokyo — hundreds of tiny bars, from karaoke dens to dance floors, all within four blocks.

spots.title: Roppongi Hills at night
spots.description: Art and altitude after dark — Mori's open-till-late museum, Keyakizaka's Tokyo Tower view, serious cocktails below.

spots.title: Harmonica Yokochō
spots.description: A postwar market maze that flips at dusk — yakitori smoke, standing bars, and jazz around the corner.

spots.title: Yūrakuchō Gādo-shita
spots.description: Izakaya packed under the brick railway arches — trains rattling overhead, highballs below. Peak after-work Tokyo.

spots.title: Miyashita Park
spots.description: A park on a mall roof with a neon food alley below — Shibuya's new-school night out.

spots.title: Shinjuku Pit Inn
spots.description: Tokyo's CBGB of jazz — a dark room where legends have played nightly since 1965.

spots.title: Hoppy Street
spots.description: Asakusa's open-air izakaya strip — beef-tendon stew, cheap hoppy highballs, and the temple glowing up the road.
